card.intuition: An advanced Git tool that lets you edit, combine (squash), delete, or reorder commits before they are finalized, allowing you to curate a perfect feature history.
card.analogy: Editing a movie: while filming (Coding), you have many 'bad takes' and mistakes. Interactive rebase is the editing room where you cut the boring parts, combine two scenes together, and fix the lighting before the audience (Code Reviewers) sees it.
when_to_use: Cleaning up 'Fix bug', 'Fix bug again' commits into one meaningful commit
Reordering commits to tell a more logical story of how a feature was built
Splitting one giant commit into smaller, easier-to-review chunks
gotchas: Rewriting history: like normal rebase, this is DANGEROUS for public branches. Only do this on your local feature branch before pushing.
Drop: if you delete a line in the interactive rebase list, that commit is PERMANENTLY deleted from your branch.

exercise.prompt: Identify the command to start an interactive rebase for the last 5 commits.
hints: It's a single dash followed by one letter
